2606.16874.title: Understanding Scam Trends and Rail Paths from Reddit Self-Disclosure Narratives
2606.16874.paper_summary: Online scam behavior is inherently multi-stage, and the lifecycle includes temporally ordered rails and events rather than isolated signals. Existing works analyze characteristics of scam types and rails, but they do not track scam trends across years. Moreover, the work on the relations between rails is hampered due to the lack of open-source datasets with annotations and coverage of different scam types. To address these gaps, we build a dataset to analyze the yearly trend of scam characteristics and rail paths using Reddit self-disclosure narratives from 2023 to 2025. We collect 21,304 posts from scam-related subreddits with at least one rail among identity, communication, platform, and payment for trend analysis by heuristic annotation. Then, we label 1,800 posts containing explicit or recoverable scam chains by an LLM-assisted method for scam path analysis. The method is evaluated with human annotation. Lastly, we run a topic model on the comments of the posts to analyze the community support behavior. The results reveal that scam processes are predominantly multi-rail. Across years, different scam types and rail components dominate. Different scam types vary systematically in path complexity. Reddit support behaviors have become more detailed over time. This work supports synthetic scam chain data simulation and AI-related scam risk assessment, though findings may not generalise to other platforms.
2606.16874.paper_summary_zh: 線上詐騙行為本質上是多階段的，其生命週期包括時間上有序的軌道和事件，而非孤立的信號。現有的研究分析了詐騙類型和軌道的特徵，但並未追蹤多年的詐騙趨勢。此外，由於缺乏帶有註釋的開源數據集以及對不同詐騙類型的覆蓋，軌道之間的關係研究受到阻礙。為了解決這些空白，我們建立了一個數據集，以分析2023年至2025年間詐騙特徵和軌道路徑的年度趨勢。我們從與詐騙相關的子版塊中收集了21,304篇帖子，這些帖子至少包含身份、通信、平台和支付中的一條軌道，以進行啟發式註釋的趨勢分析。然後，我們使用LLM輔助的方法標記了1,800篇包含明確或可恢復詐騙鏈的帖子，以進行詐騙路徑分析。該方法經過人類註釋的評估。最後，我們對這些帖子的評論運行主題模型，以分析社區支持行為。結果顯示，詐騙過程主要是多軌道的。在不同年份中，不同的詐騙類型和軌道組件佔主導地位。不同的詐騙類型在路徑複雜性上系統性地變化。隨著時間的推移，Reddit的支持行為變得更加詳細。這項工作支持合成詐騙鏈數據模擬和與AI相關的詐騙風險評估，儘管研究結果可能無法推廣到其他平台。
2606.16874.author: Yangjun Zhang et.al.
2606.16874.authors: Yangjun Zhang, Mirko Bottarelli, Mark Hooper, Carsten Maple
